name: Broad bean / faba bean (Vicia faba), mature seed (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw mature seed (fresh weight, ~11% moisture)

components.name: starch
components.source: Dhull et al. 2022 Legume Science doi:10.1002/leg3.129; feedstuff review PMC8441412 (mean 360 g/kg DM)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source; ~360 g/kg DM ≈ 33 g/100g at seed moisture; hydrolysed to glucose by amylases

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Multari sugar surveys; ScienceDirect 030881468690004X; mean ~2.4% DW
components.evidence: Principal free disaccharide in mature seed; free glucose/fructose are negligible in mature seed

components.name: verbascose
components.evidence: Principal alpha-galactoside (RFO); fermentable by microbes with alpha-galactosidase

components.name: stachyose
components.evidence: Raffinose-family oligosaccharide; alpha-galactoside

components.name: raffinose
components.evidence: Raffinose-family oligosaccharide

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose is trace/negligible in the MATURE seed (declines during maturation); present only in immature/green pods

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free fructose trace/negligible in mature seed

components.name: total sugars (USDA)
components.evidence: USDA total sugars 5.7 g reconciles with sucrose+verbascose+stachyose+raffinose (~6 g)

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: cellulose/hemicellulose/pectin; largely non-fermentable except by fiber-degraders

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: MDPI Plants 2023 PMC9967272 (relative 18.5-28.6 x10^-3 mol/L in extract)
components.evidence: Most abundant organic acid detected in mature seed; absolute mg/100g not reported (relative quantification only)

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: Detected organic acid; relative quantification only

components.name: gluconic acid
components.evidence: Detected organic acid; relative quantification only

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Detected organic acid; TCA-cycle carbon source; absolute amount not reported. Citric/succinic/fumaric not detected among primary metabolites.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: high-protein legume; globulins ~61%, albumins ~20%

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: protein-bound + free

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: most abundant EAA-adjacent residue

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: sulfur amino acid (limiting)

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: most abundant amino acid

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: legume strength; high lysine

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: limiting sulfur amino acid

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: (FooDB listed D-serine in error; the seed residue is L-serine)

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: dominant fatty acid (low-fat seed, ~1.5 g total lipid)

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: main saturated FA

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: most abundant mineral

components.name: folate (B9, total)
components.evidence: very high folate

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: from provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene, beta-cryptoxanthin), NOT preformed retinol

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: present, not quantified in USDA panel

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: provitamin-A carotenoid; genuine, contributes to vitamin A RAE

components.name: beta-cryptoxanthin
components.evidence: provitamin-A carotenoid; genuine

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: animal sterol, biologically implausible in a plant seed; FooDB predicted. Plant sterols (beta-sitosterol, campesterol, stigmasterol) are the real seed sterols and are kept as uncovered

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; plants supply provitamin-A carotenoids not retinol. USDA vitamin A here derives from carotenoids

remove.name: Pentadecanoic acid
remove.reason: odd-chain C15:0 fatty acid characteristic of ruminant/dairy fat; implausible/negligible in a legume seed, FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Heptanoate
remove.reason: C7:0 short odd-chain fatty acid; implausible in bean seed lipid, FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer is a mis-assignment; the seed protein residue and free pool is L-serine (retained in components)

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, unquantified FooDB entry; not a growth-defining nutrient of this food

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion, unquantified trace; FooDB noise (retain only as ultra-trace if a B12-cofactor need is modeled)

missing_added: verbascose, stachyose, raffinose (RFO alpha-galactosides) with amounts
organic acids: oxalic, lactic, gluconic, malic (were entirely absent; relative amounts only)
quantitative amino-acid profile (18 amino acids, USDA)
quantitative minerals (Fe, Mg, P, K, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se) and vitamins (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9/C/E/K, choline) with amounts
corrected fatty acids (linoleic dominant, palmitic, oleic, ALA) with amounts
keep_uncovered: L-DOPA (levodopa, up to ~0.4% DW ≈ 280-440 mg/100g; defining Vicia faba metabolite)
vicine (~290-610 mg/100g DM) and convicine (~63-168 mg/100g DM) — favism glycosides, genuine
catechin, epigallocatechin, gallocatechin, procyanidins B1/B2/B3/B4/C1 (genuine condensed tannins)
kaempferol/quercetin/myricetin glycosides, genistein, glycitein, isoflavones (genuine flavonoids)
pipecolic acid, wyerone, p-coumaric/ferulic acid, prodelphinidin B3, geraldone, butein, astragalin (genuine phytochemicals, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170343 (Broadbeans/fava beans, mature seeds, raw)
Dhull et al. 2022, Legume Science, doi:10.1002/leg3.129 (starch, sugars review)
Faba bean feedstuff review PMC8441412 (amino acids g/kg DM, oligosaccharides)
Multari et al. mono/oligosaccharide survey, ScienceDirect 030881468690004X
Carbohydrate composition of mature/immature faba seeds, ScienceDirect S0889157516300679
Genetic and metabolite variability in Vicia faba, MDPI Plants 2023, PMC9967272 (organic acids)
Vicine/convicine/L-DOPA distribution, ScienceDirect S2772391724000148; Springer doi:10.1007/s00217-008-0876-0
notes: Curated as the mature raw seed. Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~33 g/100g), followed by sucrose (~2.2 g) plus the raffinose-family alpha-galactosides verbascose/stachyose/raffinose (~3.8 g combined); free glucose and fructose are negligible in mature seed. High protein (26 g) makes the amino-acid pool a major nitrogen/carbon source (Glu, Asp, Arg dominant). Organic acids (oxalic > lactic ~ gluconic > malic; no citric/succinic/fumaric detected) were entirely missing from the FooDB profile and are added, but only relative quantification exists so absolute amounts are null. Removed animal/predicted noise (cholesterol, retinol, arsenate, odd-chain C15:0/C7:0 fatty acids, D-serine mis-assignment).
